member.name: Nick Timothy
member.synopsis: Nick Timothy is the Conservative MP for West Suffolk , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Shadow Secretary of State for Justice.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisons: Libraries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, to provide a list of books available to prisoners in the library at HMP Highpoint, HMP Belmarsh, HMP Manchester, and HMP Wormwood Scrubs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the library services at HMP Belmarsh, HMP Manchester and HMP Wormwood Scrubs are contracted out to the local authority, the library catalogues for those prisons are not held by the Ministry of Justice. The catalogue for HMP Highpoint is attached.

writtenQuestions.heading: Legal Aid Agency: Cybercrime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, a) what impact the 2025 cyber-attack on the Legal Aid Agency has had on data collection and publication, and b) what his Department is doing in response.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a precaution following the identification of the criminal attack on the Legal Aid Agency’s (LAA) digital portal, some of the LAA’s digital services were taken offline. This constrained the LAA’s ability to centrally collect and report on data across al...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The First-tier Tribunal (Social Entitlement Chamber) comprises three separate tribunal jurisdictions: Social Security and Child Support (SSCS); Criminal Injuries Compensation (CIC); and Asylum Support (AST).The data requested for CIC and AST are not held ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Defence: Social Media
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how much has the Department spent on influencers in aggregate since 4 July 2024; and on which campaigns and programmes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 4 July 2024 to 4 July 2026, the Ministry of Justice has spent £176,140 on influencer activity.This expenditure has supported business-critical and priority campaigns, including recruitment campaigns for essential frontline roles across His Majesty's...
